description: One row per (Case, EVA-relevant field) capturing WHERE a value came from and its review state. Phase-1 §4 modeling decision: a SEPARATE table with rows joined to the Case (NOT the prototype's embedded FieldProvenance) -- chosen because it supports per-source history, conflict rows, and audit. The Code App field adapter maps these rows <-> the prototype's embedded EvaField shape.

lifecycle.note: Write-through on every EVA-relevant Case field. Staged (not in the M1 four) but actively written by the M1 parser/enrichment/staff writers.

primaryColumn.description: Derived label (case + fieldName) for the row.

columns.description: Logical name of the EVA field this provenance describes (e.g. 'workProvider', 'mileage') -- the EVA_FIELD_ORDER key, so the adapter can join to the Case's cr1bd_eva* column.
The value as sourced (so conflicting sources can each keep their own value before staff resolve).
staff | pdf_extraction | email_text | corpus | ai | dvla_dvsa | document_ai | azure_vision | web_lookup | whatsapp | manual_upload.
Human-readable origin, e.g. 'Instruction PDF p.2' or 'Principals corpus'.
Machine reference back to the source (e.g. evidence id, document offset, lookup id).
Optional 0..1 confidence (parser / AI fields). Omit for deterministic sources.
not_required | needs_review | reviewed | conflict. Conflict rule: enrichment differing from a non-empty value -> conflict, never silent overwrite.
Staff who reviewed (set with sourceType=staff edits).

notes: Separate table (not embedded) so a field can carry multiple source rows + conflict rows with independent history.
fieldName uses the prototype EVA_FIELD_ORDER keys (camelCase domain keys), which the adapter maps to the Case cr1bd_eva* logical columns.
Writers (Phase 1): parser -> pdf_extraction; email body -> email_text; DVSA -> dvla_dvsa; domain/corpus match -> corpus; staff edit -> staff (reviewState=reviewed).
